description: Build and publish backend and frontend container images to GitHub Container Registry via GitHub Actions with CI quality gates

userStories.title: Create GitHub Actions publish workflow skeleton
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a single workflow file that defines the job graph so subsequent stories can add job content to it.
acceptanceCriteria: File .github/workflows/publish.yml is created
Workflow triggers on push to main and on push of tags matching v*.*.*
Workflow defines four empty jobs: test-backend, test-frontend, publish-backend, publish-frontend
publish-backend and publish-frontend each declare needs: [test-backend, test-frontend]
Workflow-level permissions set: contents: read, packages: write
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend test gate job
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the test-backend job to run the Django test suite so broken code is never published.
acceptanceCriteria: test-backend job checks out the repo
Job sets up Python with version pinned to match backend/Dockerfile
Job runs: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
Job runs: cd backend && python manage.py test
Workflow fails (publish jobs do not run) if tests fail
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend type-check gate job
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the test-frontend job to run vue-tsc so type errors are caught before publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: test-frontend job checks out the repo
Job sets up Node with version pinned to match frontend/Dockerfile
Job runs: cd frontend && npm ci && npm run type-check
Workflow fails (publish jobs do not run) if type-check fails
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build and publish backend image to GHCR
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the publish-backend job to build and push the backend image to GHCR after CI passes.
acceptanceCriteria: publish-backend job logs in to GHCR using GITHUB_TOKEN
Job uses docker/setup-buildx-action to enable BuildKit
Job uses docker/metadata-action to compute tags: latest + sha-<short-sha> on main push; additionally 1.2.3 / 1.2 / 1 on semver tag (v prefix stripped via flavor)
Image name follows pattern: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/memorybank-backend (lowercase)
Job uses docker/build-push-action with context: backend, cache-from: type=gha, cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
push: true only when not a pull_request event
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build and publish frontend image to GHCR
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the publish-frontend job to build and push the frontend image to GHCR after CI passes.
acceptanceCriteria: publish-frontend job mirrors publish-backend configuration
Image name: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/memorybank-frontend (lowercase)
Job uses docker/build-push-action with context: frontend
Same tagging rules as publish-backend (latest, sha-<short-sha>, semver)
GHA layer cache enabled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: docker-compose.ghcr.yml for operators
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a ready-to-use Compose file that pulls published images so I can deploy without cloning the repo.
acceptanceCriteria: File docker-compose.ghcr.yml is created at the repo root
References ghcr.io/<owner>/memorybank-backend:latest and ghcr.io/<owner>/memorybank-frontend:latest
Contains no build: keys (pull-only)
Accepts environment variables: SQLITE_PATH, MEDIA_ROOT, CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS (same as docker-compose.yml)
Mounts data/db and data/media volumes identically to docker-compose.yml
File passes: docker compose -f docker-compose.ghcr.yml config
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: README deploy section for operators
userStories.description: As an operator, I want clear deployment instructions in the README so I can run MemoryBank from published images without reading source code.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md gains a ## Deploy section distinct from the existing developer quick-start
Section shows commands to run the stack with docker-compose.ghcr.yml
Section documents SQLITE_PATH, MEDIA_ROOT, and CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS with their defaults
Section links to the GHCR package pages for both images
Existing README content is not removed or duplicated
Typecheck passes
